---
title: "Using useFetcher, useFetchers, and useRevalidator"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Using useFetcher, useFetchers, and useRevalidator}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Overview

React Router v7 provides hooks and components for managing data fetching outside
of normal navigation:

| Function | Role |
|---|---|
| `FetcherForm()` | A `<form>` bound to a fetcher — submits without navigating |
| `useFetcher()` | Read a fetcher's `state` and `data` |
| `useFetchers()` | Read the states of all active fetchers |
| `RevalidatorButton()` | A `<button>` that re-runs the current route's loader |
| `useRevalidator()` | Read the revalidation `state` |

These only work inside a data router
(`RouterProvider()`, `createHashRouter()`, or `createMemoryRouter()`).

---

## `FetcherForm()` and `useFetcher()`

### The `FetcherForm` / `useFetcher` pair

`Form()` triggers a page-level navigation (changing the URL and running the
destination route's loader). `FetcherForm()` does the same data fetch or
action **without navigating**: the URL, scroll position, and current route
remain unchanged.

`useFetcher()` reads the state of a specific fetcher. The two functions are
linked by a shared `fetcherKey` string.

```r
# Form side — submit to /search without navigating
FetcherForm(
  fetcherKey = "my-search",
  method = "get",
  action = "/search",
  tags$input(type = "search", name = "q"),
  tags$button(type = "submit", "Search")
)

# Display side — read state and data from the same fetcher
useFetcher(tags$span(), fetcherKey = "my-search", selector = "state")
useFetcher(tags$span(), fetcherKey = "my-search", selector = "data.count")
```

### `FetcherForm()` arguments

| Argument | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| `...` | yes | Child elements (inputs, buttons, etc.) and HTML attributes |
| `method` | no | `"get"` (default) or `"post"` |
| `action` | no | Route path to submit to |
| `fetcherKey` | no | Shared key to link with `useFetcher()` calls |

### `useFetcher()` arguments

| Argument | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| `into` | yes | Component that receives the value |
| `as` | no | Prop to inject into. Defaults to `"children"` |
| `selector` | no | Field from the fetcher object. Defaults to `"state"` |
| `fetcherKey` | no | Must match the `fetcherKey` in `FetcherForm()` |

The fetcher object fields available via `selector`:

| Selector | Value |
|---|---|
| `"state"` | `"idle"` / `"loading"` / `"submitting"` |
| `"data"` | Full response from the loader or action |
| `"data.myField"` | Nested field from the response |

### Full example: inline search

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(htmltools)
library(jsonlite)

people_json <- jsonlite::toJSON(
  data.frame(name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie", "David", "Eve")),
  dataframe = "rows",
  auto_unbox = TRUE
)

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    element = div(
      tags$h2("Inline search"),
      FetcherForm(
        fetcherKey = "search",
        method = "get",
        action = "/search",
        tags$input(type = "search", name = "q", placeholder = "Search..."),
        tags$button(type = "submit", "Search")
      ),
      tags$p("Status: ", useFetcher(tags$strong(), fetcherKey = "search", selector = "state")),
      tags$p("Matches: ", useFetcher(tags$strong(), fetcherKey = "search", selector = "data.count")),
      Outlet()
    ),
    Route(index = TRUE, element = div()),
    Route(
      path = "search",
      loader = JS(sprintf(
        "({ request }) => {
          const q = new URL(request.url).searchParams.get('q') || '';
          const db = %s;
          const hits = db.filter(r => r.name.toLowerCase().includes(q.toLowerCase()));
          return { count: hits.length, results: hits };
        }",
        people_json
      )),
      element = div()
    )
  )
)
```

---

## `useFetchers()`

`useFetchers()` returns all currently active fetchers. This is useful for a
**global loading indicator** that shows whenever any background fetch is in
progress, regardless of which component started it.

### Arguments

| Argument | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| `into` | yes | Component that receives the value |
| `as` | no | Prop to inject into. Defaults to `"children"` |
| `selector` | no | Field to extract from **each** fetcher. If `NULL` (default), returns the full array as JSON |

### Example: monitor all active fetchers

```r
# Renders a JSON array of state strings whenever fetchers are active:
# e.g. '["loading","idle"]'
useFetchers(tags$code(), selector = "state")
```

When no fetchers are active the result is `"[]"`. Combine with a parent layout
route to show a site-wide spinner:

```r
Route(
  path = "/",
  element = div(
    tags$p("Active fetchers: ", useFetchers(tags$code(), selector = "state")),
    Outlet()
  ),
  # child routes with FetcherForm calls ...
)
```

---

## `RevalidatorButton()` and `useRevalidator()`

Revalidation re-runs the current route's loader **without a page-level
navigation**. This is distinct from navigation (which changes the URL) and
from fetchers (which load a different route in the background).

### `RevalidatorButton()`

`RevalidatorButton()` renders a `<button>` that calls
`revalidator.revalidate()` when clicked. It is automatically disabled while
revalidation is in progress.

```r
RevalidatorButton(
  "Refresh data",
  style = "padding: 6px 14px; cursor: pointer;"
)
```

### `useRevalidator()` arguments

| Argument | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| `into` | yes | Component that receives the value |
| `as` | no | Prop to inject into. Defaults to `"children"` |
| `selector` | no | Field from the revalidator. Defaults to `"state"` |

The revalidator `state` is `"idle"` at rest and `"loading"` while the loader
is re-running.

### Full example: manual refresh button

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(htmltools)

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    loader = JS(
      "async () => {
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1000));
        return { time: new Date().toLocaleTimeString() };
      }"
    ),
    element = div(
      tags$p("Loaded at: ",        useLoaderData(tags$strong(), selector = "time")),
      tags$p("Revalidation state: ", useRevalidator(tags$span())),
      RevalidatorButton("Refresh", style = "padding: 6px 14px; cursor: pointer;")
    ),
    Route(index = TRUE, element = div())
  )
)
```

---

## Comparison

| Hook / Component | Triggers | State visible via | URL changes? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Form()` + `useNavigation()` | page navigation | `useNavigation(selector = "state")` | Yes |
| `FetcherForm()` + `useFetcher()` | background fetch | `useFetcher(selector = "state")` | No |
| `RevalidatorButton()` + `useRevalidator()` | loader re-run | `useRevalidator()` | No |
